commit.message:
Add FiiO μBTR button listener daemon

Adds fiio_listener.py — an evdev daemon that:
- Finds the FiiO μBTR AVRCP input device by name
- Grabs it exclusively to prevent HFP/X11 interference
- Toggles voice-to-text on each button press
- Forces FiiO to A2DP profile (a2dp-sink-sbc) on startup
- Sets UGREEN USB mic as default audio input on startup

Setup: run as a systemd user service (fiio-listener.service).
This allows using the FiiO physical button as an alternative
to the Alt+R keyboard shortcut.

chore: release 1.53.0 (#306)

The §29 Phase 3 migration (8a81e9e) changed nav items from <a> links
to <button> elements, but E2E tests still used getByRole('link').
This caused all navigation-related E2E tests to time out at 31.5s.

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Spec updated: §29.5.10 marked as delivered with implementation details.
Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit
- Enable/disable audit
- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:
- New: prefetchManager.ts — manages loader registry, queue, budget, cancel
- types.ts: add prefetchModules?: string[] to ModuleManifest + DTO + validation
- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches
  Agents and Sessions

- moduleRegistry.ts: cancel queued prefetches on disable (both own and hinted)
- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

Add frontend-only metrics collector that tracks per-module lifecycle

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)
- SqliteModuleVisibilityStore with transactional replace (Infrastructure)
- Migration 014: module_visibility table
- GET/PUT /api/module/visibility/project/{projectId} endpoints
- Audit logging for visibility changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/moduleRegistry.ts — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/modules/ModuleContainer.tsx — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests
- docs/spec/29-module-system.md — updated §29.8.5 to reflect impl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.6 project deletion cleanup (#267)

Implement project-scoped data cleanup when a project is deleted.

Backend:
- Add DeleteForProjectAsync to IModuleSettingsStore, IPluginSettingsStore,
  and IModuleVisibilityStore domain interfaces
- Implement in SQLite stores with LIKE-escaped project ID matching
- New IProjectCleanupService orchestrator calls all three stores
- DELETE /api/module/project/{projectId} endpoint with 207 on partial failure
- Audit logging for cleanup events

Frontend:
- cleanupProjectModuleData() API client function
- projectCache.clearProject() already existed for in-memory cleanup

Safety:
- SQL LIKE wildcards in projectId are escaped to prevent cross-project deletion
- Partial store failures return HTTP 207 with details (not masked as 200 OK)
- Each store cleanup is independent — one failure doesn't block others

The project system does not yet have a project entity with CRUD operations.
This provides the cleanup building blocks ready to wire when project
deletion is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add disposeAll() to ModuleRegistry that disposes modules in reverse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.10 bundle integrity — SHA-256 ETag and dev-mode verification

Backend (ModuleController.GetBundle):
- Compute SHA-256 of bundle file contents, return as ETag header
- Handle If-None-Match for 304 Not Modified (proper EntityTagHeaderValue parsing)
- Read file once to guarantee hash/body consistency (bundles capped at 2 MB)

Frontend (modules/bundleIntegrity.ts):
- fetchBundleWithIntegrity() fetches bundle and verifies SHA-256 in dev mode
- Uses SubtleCrypto.digest for client-side hash computation
- Skips verification in production (browser HTTP cache uses ETag natively)
- Re-exported from api/client.ts for consumer convenience

Tests:

* fix: Discord plugin UX and settings double-encoding

- Rename "Guild ID" to "Server ID" in plugin settings label/description

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement §29.7.5 timer & subscription cleanup (#270)

* feat: implement §29.5.8 settings migration on version change

When a module's settingsSchema changes between versions, the host now
detects the version change and migrates settings:

- Track module versions in module_state.version (migration 015)
- On startup, compare manifest version to stored version
- Remove stored values incompatible with new schema constraints
  (type changes, out-of-range numbers, removed select options)
- Handle both global and project-scoped settings (project:{id}:{key})
- Preserve unmapped fields as-is (keys not in new schema)
- Do NOT eagerly persist defaults — preserves §29.5.9 provenance
- On failure: log warning, skip version update, retry next startup
- Secret values skipped (masked, can't validate)

Domain: IModuleSettingsStore gains GetVersionAsync, SetVersionAsync,
DeleteKeyAsync. Infrastructure: SqliteModuleSettingsStore implements them.
ModuleDiscoveryService runs migration after scan.

22 new tests (16 migration behavior + 6 store-level).

mockResolvedValue) triggered state updates via .then(setState) in microtasks

to flush pending microtasks within an act() scope.

commit.message:
pcie: stop resetting bridge, use firmware's DMA config

Firmware (start4.elf) configures the PCIe bridge with correct BAR2
inbound windows, UBUS remap, SCB_SIZE, and link training. Our bridge
reset was destroying this config, forcing us to reprogram from scratch
— but we couldn't recreate the dual-path DMA translation that both
xHCI (PCIe 0x00) and MACB (PCIe 0x10 via RP1 EP) need.

Now pcie_init() just:
- Verifies firmware left the link up
- Sets outbound ATU window for RP1 MMIO
- Enables bus master
- Dumps firmware's actual BAR2/UBUS/MISC_CTRL config
- Initializes AER

Also adds BAR2 register dump inside macb_init (where UART is live)
to capture the firmware's actual DMA translation config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix notes text not wrapping before button area stretches (#30)

Long notes text was pushing the right-side container wider than the
rating buttons row, instead of word-wrapping within the buttons' width.

The right-side container was a plain `<div>` whose width was determined
by its widest child. When notes exceeded the buttons row width, the
entire container grew.

- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Make the right-side container `flex w-min
flex-col items-end` — `w-min` (`width: min-content`) constrains the
column width to the `shrink-0` rating buttons' intrinsic width, forcing
note text to word-wrap within that width
- **`note-editor.tsx`**: Add `self-stretch` to both display and editing
containers so they fill the column width, and add `break-words` to the
note paragraph for safety with very long words

  hardening (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, PrivateTmp)

commit.message:
fix: protect directive not revealing when wrapping tabs

The protect client.js used getElementsByTagName('input')[0] to find
the password input, which grabbed the first input in the entire subtree.
When protect wrapped tabs, this selected a tab radio input instead of
the password input, so event listeners were attached to the wrong
element and password entry was silently ignored.

Scope both selectors to direct children using :scope queries:
- Input: el.querySelector(':scope > .password-input input')
- Hidden: el.querySelector(':scope > .hidden')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove erroneous PR subtraction from issue counts; clamp negatives in display

The issues.listForRepo API returns only issues (not PRs) in its
response, so subtracting PR counts produced large negative issue values
(e.g. -468, -619) which Chart.js rendered as bars extending to the
left, creating an unintended butterfly/diverging chart appearance.

Changes:
- collectIssueCounts: drop the two pulls.list calls and remove the
  subtraction; use the raw issues API counts directly (+ Math.max(0)
  guard for safety)
- build-pages.ts: clamp r.issues.open / r.issues.closed to >= 0 when
  aggregating totals and building topRepos, so any existing cached or
  fixture data with negative values is rendered correctly
- issues.test.ts: update tests to reflect the new single-API approach

commit.message:
feat(wisher-details): Add wisher details feature with type-safe routing (#65)

* feat(wisher-details): Add wisher details screen and view model

Implement the wisher details feature with:
- WisherDetailsScreen: Pure UI presentation layer with loading/error states
- WisherDetailsViewModel: Business logic for loading wisher data by ID
- Type-safe navigation: Routes.wisherDetails.buildPath(id: id) helper
- Full MVVM integration with Provider dependency injection

The screen displays a specific wisher's profile when navigated from the
wishers list, with proper loading states and fallback messaging. Uses the
cached wisher repository for data retrieval.

Architecture follows CLAUDE.md standards with abstract contracts and
constructor injection for testability.
